content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed all three amendments on correctness and safety. I agree with accepting the two regressions that can corrupt terminal-state reconciliation (fr-9-fc2b4c4f, fr-9-5fed1ae0) and reject the missing-PID-as-stale change (fr-9-08c4309f) due to a concrete ownership/race safety regression.

## Votes

- `fr-9-fc2b4c4f`: Accept
  This is a real correctness issue: bypassing journal-first reconciliation after lease claim can overwrite a durable terminal outcome with a synthetic interruption/failure state when the prior orchestrator already wrote run_completed/run_failed but missed snapshot persist. The fix correctly preserves durable terminal truth and prevents illegal resume of terminal runs.
- `fr-9-08c4309f`: Reject
  Treating any fresh lease without pid as stale is unsafe because it can enable another CLI to reclaim ownership while a legacy orchestrator may still be actively managing the same run. This creates a real double-owner/ state corruption risk; a safer approach needs stronger stale proof than missing pid alone in mixed-version/legacy contexts.
- `fr-9-5fed1ae0`: Accept
  Filtering only by run_id is insufficient when attempts reuse run_id across resume. Older terminal events can be replayed against a live resumed attempt and force a Running snapshot to terminal incorrectly. Attempt-aware boundary checks at run_started/run_resumed are required to avoid false terminal transitions.
